title: Vintage Gas Pump Cabinet with Side Bookshelves
summary: A tall storage cabinet styled as an old gas pump, flanked by two matching bookshelves — a single door and a round "gauge" cutout sell the look.
description:
This is a real storage cabinet — a face-framed bookshelf box with a single hinged door — dressed up as a vintage gas pump, flanked by two smaller bookshelf towers built as separate, identical units. Building the side bookshelves as their own boxes rather than one continuous wide shelf is what lets the center cabinet stand slightly proud of them and read as the "pump" against two supporting towers.

The theming details are what make this project: a round disc cut from 1x12 becomes the pump's gauge face, a length of black flex tubing becomes the hose, and a scrap-cut handle finishes the illusion. None of that is structural — build the cabinet and shelves solidly first, then treat the gas-pump details as trim you add at the end, so a mistake on the fun stuff never costs you the carcass.

tools.note: Cuts the round gauge-face disc and the decorative handle.

cutList.note: Two per side bookshelf, two side bookshelves total.
Cut into an 11" circle with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Build the main cabinet box
steps.body: Build the main cabinet using pocket holes or nails and glue at every joint — both approaches work here because every joint gets reinforced by the back and face frame in later steps. Attach the back with 3/4" nails and wood glue, nailing into every shelf edge as you go.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame separately, flat, then attach the completed frame to the front of the cabinet with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Build both side bookshelves
steps.body: Build the two side bookshelves as separate boxes. Nail and glue is fine since each will be reinforced by attachment to the main cabinet, though pocket holes are worth using on the top and bottom 1x2 backs where extra strength matters.

steps.title: Attach the side bookshelves and trim
steps.body: Attach the side bookshelves to the main cabinet, working from the back forward, cutting trim pieces to fit as you go rather than pre-cutting everything. Nail on with 1-1/4" nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the gauge, base feet, and door
steps.body: Cut the round gauge-face disc with a jigsaw and mount it to the top of the cabinet as the pump's dial — this is the single detail that reads "gas pump" from across the room, so take care getting it centered. Attach the base feet. The door is simply the 1x12 board, hung on with the hinges and magnetic clasp.

steps.title: Add the hose, handle, and finish details
steps.body: Mount the 2-hole strap and run the black flex tubing from it to suggest the pump's hose. Cut a handle shape from 1x4 scrap, paint it, and attach it to the door. Fill nail holes with wood filler, sand, and finish or paint as desired — a hand-lettered or stenciled sign on top completes the look.

images.alt: Storage cabinet styled as a vintage gas pump with matching side bookshelves
